name: Reimbursement Forensics (ReiFor)
description: Compute the reimbursable total (USD cents) for each travel reimbursement case from a solver-visible evidence bundle: policy excerpt, exchange-rate table, receipts, and emails.
capability_claim: Evaluates multi-document consistency reasoning, careful rule interpretation, numerical auditing, and robust handling of adversarial edge cases in messy-but-finite evidence bundles.

closest_existing_benchmarks.name: DocVQA / document QA
closest_existing_benchmarks.reason: Also requires extracting information from documents, but ReiFor requires full-policy adjudication and exact numeric reconciliation.

closest_existing_benchmarks.name: GAIA / tool-augmented reasoning
closest_existing_benchmarks.reason: Both reward tool use, but ReiFor is fully self-contained and deterministically graded from a fixed bundle.

closest_existing_benchmarks.name: SWE-bench (reconciliation flavor)
closest_existing_benchmarks.reason: Shares an 'audit and verify' flavor, but ReiFor is not code debugging and has no hidden tests; everything needed is in the documents.

grading_method: Exact match on integer USD cents.
input_format: JSONL items pointing to a solver bundle with text exhibits and shared policy/rate tables.
output_format: JSONL lines with fields {id, answer} where answer is an integer string (USD cents).
